channel.title: Pablo Bernabeu
channel.description: Recent content in conceptual replication on Pablo Bernabeu
channel.copyright: Pablo Bernabeu, 2015—{year}. Licence: [CC BY 4.0](https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/4.0/). Email: pcbernabeu@gmail.com. Cookies only used by third-party systems such as [Disqus](https://help.disqus.com/en/articles/1717155-use-of-cookies).

item.title: Dutch modality exclusivity norms for 336 properties and 411 concepts
item.description:
Study at a glance

graph TD A["42 Dutch speakers"] --> B["Rate auditory, haptic and visual strength
of 336 properties + 411 concepts"] B --> C["Mean ratings per word
(acceptable reliability)"] C --> D["Derived measures:
modality exclusivity, perceptual strength"] C --> E["Linked corpus variables:
length, frequency, distinctiveness,
concreteness, age of acquisition"] D --> F["Replicate Lynott and
Connell (2009, 2013)"] F --> G["Uni-, bi- and tri-modal words;
vision most prevalent"] F --> H["Vision and touch related;
audition more independent"] F --> I["Properties more perceptual
than concepts"] F --> J["Sound symbolism: auditory strength
best predicts lexical properties"]

Reference

Bernabeu, P. (2018). Dutch modality exclusivity norms for 336 properties and 411 concepts. PsyArXiv. https://doi.org/10.31234/osf.io/s2c5h

Related podcast

Created using NotebookLM, with all the benefits and blind spots of human editing.

item.title: Modality switch effects emerge early and increase throughout conceptual processing: Evidence from ERPs
item.description:
Study at a glance

graph TD A["46 participants verify
property-concept word relations"] --> B["Modality of consecutive
trials manipulated"] B --> C["Auditory-to-visual switch"] B --> D["Haptic-to-visual switch"] B --> E["Visual-to-visual no-switch"] C --> F["ERPs time-locked
to first word onset"] D --> F E --> F F --> G["Switch effect: more negative
amplitudes for switches"] G --> H["Significant in four windows from 160 to 750 ms,
stronger posteriorly and after 350 ms"]

Reference

Bernabeu, P., Willems, R. M., & Louwerse, M. M. (2017). Modality switch effects emerge early and increase throughout conceptual processing: Evidence from ERPs. In G. Gunzelmann, A. Howes, T. Tenbrink, & E. J. Davelaar (Eds.), Proceedings of the 39th Annual Conference of the Cognitive Science Society (pp. 1629-1634). Austin, TX: Cognitive Science Society. https://doi.org/10.31234/osf.io/a5pcz

Related podcasts

Created using NotebookLM, with all the benefits and blind spots of human editing.

Conference poster with additional analyses
